title: Config
description: Represents the configuration for the Sprocket CLI tool.

common.description: Common configuration options for all commands.

modules.description: Configuration for the module system (`[modules]` section).

sort_imports.description: Whether to sort import statements alphabetically.

trailing_commas.description: Whether to add trailing commas to multiline lists.

NewlineStyle.description: The newline style to use when formatting.

oneOf.description: Use the native newline style of the platform.
Use Unix-style newlines (`\n`).
Use Windows-style newlines (`\r\n`).

CheckConfig.description: Represents the configuration for the Sprocket `check` and `lint` commands.

except.description: Rule IDs to except from running.

deny_warnings.description: Causes the command to fail if any warnings are reported.

deny_notes.description: Causes the command to fail if any notes or warnings are reported.

all_lint_rules.description:
Enable all lint rules, even those outside the default set.

This cannot be `true` while `only_lint_tags` is populated.

only_lint_tags.description:
Set of lint tags to opt into. Leave this empty to use the default set of
tags.

filter_lint_tags.description: Set of lint tags to filter out of the enabled lint rules.

baseline.description: Path to the diagnostic baseline file.

allowed_runtime_keys.description:
List of keys to ignore in the [`ExpectedRuntimeKeys`] lint.

##### Example

```toml
allowed_runtime_keys = ["foo"]
```

[`ExpectedRuntimeKeys`]: crate::rules::ExpectedRuntimeKeysRule

allowed_names.description:
List of names to ignore in the [`SnakeCase`] and [`DeclarationName`]
lints.

##### Example

```toml
allowed_names = ["Foo", "counter_int"]
```

[`SnakeCase`]: crate::rules::SnakeCaseRule
[`DeclarationName`]: crate::rules::DeclarationNameRule

AnalyzerConfig.description: Represents the configuration for the Sprocket `analyzer` command.

except.description: Rule IDs to except from running.

RunConfig.description: Represents the configuration for the Sprocket `run` command.

output_dir.description:
The output directory (default: `./out`).

Individual runs are stored at `<output_dir>/runs/<target>/<timestamp>/`.

events_capacity.description:
The capacity of the events channel used to display progress statistics.

If the number of progress events being generated outpaces Sprocket's
ability to read the events for displaying the progress statistics and
the channel reaches its capacity, Sprocket will stop displaying progress
statistics. This is most likely to occur when Sprocket has concurrently
queued up a large number of tasks.

Increasing the capacity will increase the size of the memory allocation
made by the events channel.

The default is `5000`.

WdlEngineConfig.description:
Represents WDL evaluation configuration.
By default, serialization of [`Config`] will not redact the values of secrets. Use the [`Config::redact`] method prior to serialization to redact secrets.

backend.description: The name of the backend to use.

backends.description:
Task execution backends configuration.

If the collection is empty and `backend` has the default value, the
engine default backend is used.

suppress_env_specific_output.description:
(Experimental) Avoid environment-specific output; default is `false`.

If this option is `true`, selected error messages and log output will
avoid emitting environment-specific output such as absolute paths
and system resource counts.

This is largely meant to support "golden testing" where a test's success
depends on matching an expected set of outputs exactly. Cues that
help users overcome errors, such as the path to a temporary
directory or the number of CPUs available to the system, confound this
style of testing. This flag is a best-effort experimental attempt to
reduce the impact of these differences in order to allow a wider
range of golden tests to be written.

experimental_features_enabled.description:
(Experimental) Whether experimental features are enabled; default is
`false`.

Experimental features are provided to users with heavy caveats about
their stability and rough edges. Use at your own risk, but feedback
is quite welcome.

fail.description:
The failure mode for workflow or task evaluation.

A value of [`FailureMode::Slow`] will result in evaluation waiting for
executing tasks to complete upon error or interruption.

A value of [`FailureMode::Fast`] will immediately attempt to cancel
executing tasks upon error or interruption.

cache_dir.description:
The HTTP download cache location.

Defaults to an operating system specific cache directory for the user.

retries.description: The number of retries for transferring files.

parallelism.description:
The maximum parallelism for file transfers.

Defaults to the host's available parallelism.

hash_algorithm.description:
The hash algorithm to use for calculating content digests for file
uploads.

Defaults to `sha256`.

Parallelism.description: Represents the parallelism for HTTP downloads.

anyOf.description: Use the available parallelism for the host system.

concurrency.description:
The number of scatter array elements to process concurrently. Defaults to `1000`. A value of `0` is invalid. Lower values use less memory for evaluation and higher values may better saturate the task execution backend with tasks to execute for large scatters. This setting does not change how many tasks an execution backend can run concurrently, but may affect how many tasks are sent to the backend to run at a time. For example, if `concurrency` was set to 10 and we evaluate the following scatters: ```wdl scatter (i in range(100)) { call my_task } scatter (j in range(100)) { call my_task as my_task2 } ``` Here each scatter is independent and therefore there will be 20 calls (10 for each scatter) made concurrently. If the task execution backend can only execute 5 tasks concurrently, 5 tasks will execute and 15 will be "ready" to execute and waiting for an executing task to complete. If instead we evaluate the following scatters: ```wdl scatter (i in range(100)) { scatter (j in range(100)) { call my_task } } ``` Then there will be 100 calls (10*10 as 10 are made for each outer element) made concurrently. If the task execution backend can only execute 5 tasks concurrently, 5 tasks will execute and 95 will be "ready" to execute and waiting for an executing task to complete.
Warning: nested scatter statements cause exponential memory usage based on this value, as each scatter statement evaluation requires allocating new scopes for scatter array elements being processed.

retries.description:
The default maximum number of retries to attempt if a task fails.

A task's `max_retries` requirement will override this value.

container.description:
The default container to use if a container is not specified in a task's
requirements.

shell.description:
The default shell to use for tasks.
Warning: the use of a shell other than `bash` may lead to tasks that may not be portable to other execution engines. The shell must support a `-c` option to run a specific script file (i.e. an evaluated task command). Note that this option affects all task commands, so every container that is used must contain the specified shell. If using this setting causes your tasks to fail, please do not file an issue.

cpu_limit_behavior.description: The behavior when a task's `cpu` requirement cannot be met.

memory_limit_behavior.description: The behavior when a task's `memory` requirement cannot be met.

cache_dir.description:
The call cache directory to use for caching task execution results.

Defaults to an operating system specific cache directory for the user.

cache.description: The call caching mode to use for tasks.

digests.description:
The content digest mode to use.

Used as part of call caching.

excluded_cache_requirements.description:
Keys of task requirements to exclude from call cache checking.

When specified, these requirement keys will be ignored when
calculating cache keys and validating cache entries.

This can be useful for requirements that may vary between runs
but should not invalidate the cache (e.g., dynamic resource
allocation).

excluded_cache_hints.description:
Keys of task hints to exclude from call cache checking.

When specified, these hint keys will be ignored when
calculating cache keys and validating cache entries.

This can be useful for hints that may vary between runs
but should not invalidate the cache.

excluded_cache_inputs.description:
Keys of task inputs to exclude from call cache checking.

When specified, these input keys will be ignored when
calculating cache keys and validating cache entries.

This can be useful for inputs that may vary between runs
but should not affect the task's output.

Retries.description: Represents the maximum number of retries for tasks.

anyOf.description: Use the default number of retries for task execution.
Use the specified number of retries for task execution.

TaskResourceLimitBehavior.description:
The behavior when a task resource requirement, such as `cpu` or `memory`,
cannot be met.

oneOf.description:
Try executing a task with the maximum amount of the resource available
when the task's corresponding requirement cannot be met.

Do not execute a task if its corresponding requirement cannot be met.

This is the default behavior.

CallCachingMode.description: Represents the supported call caching modes.

oneOf.description:
Call caching is disabled.

The call cache is not checked and new entries are not added to the
cache.

This is the default value.

Call caching is enabled.

The call cache is checked and new entries are added to the cache.

Defaults the `cacheable` task hint to `true`.

Call caching is enabled only for tasks that explicitly have a
`cacheable` hint set to `true`.

The call cache is checked and new entries are added to the cache *only*
for tasks that have the `cacheable` hint set to `true`.

Defaults the `cacheable` task hint to `false`.

ContentDigestMode.description: Represents the supported modes for calculating content digests.

oneOf.description:
Use a strong digest for file content.

Strong digests require hashing all of the contents of a file; this may
noticeably impact performance for very large files.

This setting guarantees that a modified file will be detected.

Use a weak digest for file content.

A weak digest is based solely off of file metadata, such as size and
last modified time.

This setting cannot guarantee the detection of modified files and may
result in a modified file not causing a call cache entry to be
invalidated.

However, it is substantially faster than using a strong digest.
Use the local task execution backend.

cpu.description:
Set the number of CPUs available for task execution.

Defaults to the number of logical CPUs for the host.

The value cannot be zero or exceed the host's number of CPUs.

memory.description:
Set the total amount of memory for task execution as a unit string (e.g.
`2 GiB`).

Defaults to the total amount of memory for the host.

The value cannot be zero or exceed the host's total amount of memory.

oneOf.description: Use the Docker task execution backend.

cleanup.description:
Whether or not to remove a task's container after the task completes.

Defaults to `true`.

oneOf.description: Use the TES task execution backend.

url.description: The URL of the Task Execution Service.

auth.description: The authentication configuration for the TES backend.

inputs.description: The root cloud storage URL for storing inputs.

outputs.description: The root cloud storage URL for storing outputs.

interval.description:
The polling interval, in seconds, for checking task status.

Defaults to 1 second.

retries.description:
The number of retries after encountering an error communicating with the
TES server.

Defaults to no retries.

max_concurrency.description:
The maximum number of concurrent requests the backend will send to the
TES server.

Defaults to 10 concurrent requests.

insecure.description:
Whether or not the TES server URL may use an insecure protocol like
HTTP.

oneOf.description:
Use the experimental LSF + Apptainer task execution backend.

Requires enabling experimental features.

interval.description:
The task monitor polling interval, in seconds.

Defaults to 30 seconds.

max_concurrency.description:
The maximum number of concurrent LSF operations the backend will
perform.

This controls the maximum concurrent number of `bsub` processes the
backend will spawn to queue tasks.

Defaults to 10 concurrent operations.

default_lsf_queue.description:
Which queue, if any, to specify when submitting normal jobs to LSF.

This may be superseded by
[`short_task_lsf_queue`][Self::short_task_lsf_queue],
[`gpu_lsf_queue`][Self::gpu_lsf_queue], or
[`fpga_lsf_queue`][Self::fpga_lsf_queue] for corresponding tasks.

short_task_lsf_queue.description:
Which queue, if any, to specify when submitting [short
tasks](https://github.com/openwdl/wdl/blob/wdl-1.2/SPEC.md#short_task) to LSF.

This may be superseded by [`gpu_lsf_queue`][Self::gpu_lsf_queue] or
[`fpga_lsf_queue`][Self::fpga_lsf_queue] for tasks which require
specialized hardware.

gpu_lsf_queue.description:
Which queue, if any, to specify when submitting [tasks which require a
GPU](https://github.com/openwdl/wdl/blob/wdl-1.2/SPEC.md#hardware-accelerators-gpu-and--fpga)
to LSF.

fpga_lsf_queue.description:
Which queue, if any, to specify when submitting [tasks which require an
FPGA](https://github.com/openwdl/wdl/blob/wdl-1.2/SPEC.md#hardware-accelerators-gpu-and--fpga)
to LSF.

job_name_prefix.description:
Prefix to add to every LSF job name before the task identifier. This is
truncated as needed to satisfy the byte-oriented LSF job name limit.

bsub.description: The additional arguments to `bsub` used to queue a new task.

apptainer.description:
The configuration of Apptainer, which is used as the container runtime
on the compute nodes where LSF dispatches tasks.

Note that this will likely be replaced by an abstraction over multiple
container execution runtimes in the future, rather than being
hardcoded to Apptainer.

oneOf.description:
Use the experimental Slurm + Apptainer task execution backend.

Requires enabling experimental features.

interval.description:
The task monitor polling interval, in seconds.

Defaults to 30 seconds.

max_concurrency.description:
The maximum number of concurrent Slurm operations the backend will
perform.

This controls the maximum concurrent number of `sbatch` processes the
backend will spawn to queue tasks.

Defaults to 10 concurrent operations.

default_slurm_partition.description:
Which partition, if any, to specify when submitting normal jobs to
Slurm.

This may be superseded by
[`short_task_slurm_partition`][Self::short_task_slurm_partition],
[`gpu_slurm_partition`][Self::gpu_slurm_partition], or
[`fpga_slurm_partition`][Self::fpga_slurm_partition] for corresponding
tasks.

short_task_slurm_partition.description:
Which partition, if any, to specify when submitting [short
tasks](https://github.com/openwdl/wdl/blob/wdl-1.2/SPEC.md#short_task) to Slurm.

This may be superseded by
[`gpu_slurm_partition`][Self::gpu_slurm_partition] or
[`fpga_slurm_partition`][Self::fpga_slurm_partition] for tasks which
require specialized hardware.

gpu_slurm_partition.description:
Which partition, if any, to specify when submitting [tasks which require
a GPU](https://github.com/openwdl/wdl/blob/wdl-1.2/SPEC.md#hardware-accelerators-gpu-and--fpga)
to Slurm.

fpga_slurm_partition.description:
Which partition, if any, to specify when submitting [tasks which require
an FPGA](https://github.com/openwdl/wdl/blob/wdl-1.2/SPEC.md#hardware-accelerators-gpu-and--fpga)
to Slurm.

sbatch.description: The additional arguments to `sbatch` used to queue a new task.

job_name_prefix.description: Prefix to add to every Slurm job name before the task identifier.

apptainer.description:
The configuration of Apptainer, which is used as the container runtime
on the compute nodes where Slurm dispatches tasks.

Note that this will likely be replaced by an abstraction over multiple
container execution runtimes in the future, rather than being
hardcoded to Apptainer.

TesBackendAuthConfig.description: Represents the kind of authentication for a TES backend.

oneOf.description: Use basic authentication for the TES backend.
Use bearer token authentication for the TES backend.

SecretString.description:
Represents a secret string that is, by default, redacted for serialization.

This type is a wrapper around [`secrecy::SecretString`].

LsfQueueConfig.description:
Configuration for an LSF queue.

Each queue can optionally have per-task CPU and memory limits set so that
tasks which are too large to be scheduled on that queue will fail
immediately instead of pending indefinitely. In the future, these limits may
be populated or validated by live information from the cluster, but
for now they must be manually based on the user's understanding of the
cluster configuration.

name.description:
The name of the queue; this is the string passed to `bsub -q
<queue_name>`.

max_cpu_per_task.description: The maximum number of CPUs this queue can provision for a single task.

max_memory_per_task.description: The maximum memory this queue can provision for a single task.

AdditionalArgs.description:
Represents additional arguments to the Slurm and LSF backends.

These arguments are passed to the executable responsible for queuing a task.

args.description: The additional arguments to pass to the backend program.

conditional.description:
The conditional arguments to pass to the backend program.

The first conditional argument with an associated conditional expression
that evaluates to `true` will be passed to the backend program.

ConditionalArgs.description:
Represents a set of conditional arguments for the LSF and Slurm backends.

Conditional arguments are passed to the program responsible for queuing a
task when the associated conditional expression evaluates to `true`.

condition.description: The condition for including the arguments.

args.description: The arguments to use when the condition evaluates to `true`.

Condition.description:
Represents a condition in a conditional argument.

The expression is evaluated in a context where a task's computed `cpu`,
`memory`, `gpu`, `fpga`, and `disks` values and evaluated `hint` object are
available as variables.

The expression is type checked during configuration deserialization to
ensure it is a valid WDL expression of type `Boolean`.

ApptainerConfig.description: Configuration for the Apptainer container runtime.

executable.description:
Path to the Apptainer (or Singularity) executable.

Defaults to `"apptainer"`. Set to `"singularity"` or a full path
(e.g., `/usr/local/bin/apptainer`) if the executable is not on `PATH`
or if using Singularity instead.

image_cache_dir.description:
Path to a shared directory for caching pulled `.sif` images.

When set, pulled images are stored in this directory and shared
across runs. When unset, images are stored in a per-run directory
that is not shared.

extra_args.description:
Additional command-line arguments to pass to `apptainer exec` when
executing tasks.

SlurmPartitionConfig.description:
Configuration for a Slurm partition.

Each partition can optionally have per-task CPU and memory limits set so
that tasks which are too large to be scheduled on that partition will fail
immediately instead of pending indefinitely. In the future, these limits may
be populated or validated by live information from the cluster, but
for now they must be manually based on the user's understanding of the
cluster configuration.

name.description:
The name of the partition; this is the string passed to `sbatch
--partition=<partition_name>`.

max_cpu_per_task.description:
The maximum number of CPUs this partition can provision for a single
task.

max_memory_per_task.description: The maximum memory this partition can provision for a single task.

AzureStorageConfig.description: Represents configuration for Azure Blob Storage.

auth.description: The Azure Blob Storage authentication configuration.

AzureStorageAuthConfig.description: Represents authentication information for Azure Blob Storage.

account_name.description: The Azure Storage account name to use.

access_key.description: The Azure Storage access key to use.

S3StorageConfig.description: Represents configuration for AWS S3 storage.

region.description:
The default region to use for S3-schemed URLs (e.g.
`s3://<bucket>/<blob>`).

Defaults to `us-east-1`.

auth.description: The AWS S3 storage authentication configuration.

S3StorageAuthConfig.description: Represents authentication information for AWS S3 storage.

access_key_id.description: The AWS Access Key ID to use.

secret_access_key.description: The AWS Secret Access Key to use.

GoogleStorageConfig.description: Represents configuration for Google Cloud Storage.

auth.description: The Google Cloud Storage authentication configuration.

GoogleStorageAuthConfig.description: Represents authentication information for Google Cloud Storage.

access_key.description: The HMAC Access Key to use.

FailureMode.description:
Represents how an evaluation error or cancellation should be handled by the
engine.

oneOf.description:
When an error is encountered or evaluation is canceled, evaluation waits
for any outstanding tasks to complete.

When an error is encountered or evaluation is canceled, any outstanding
tasks that are executing are immediately canceled and evaluation waits
for cancellation to complete.

allowed_file_paths.description: Allowed file paths for file-based workflows.

allowed_urls.description: Allowed URL prefixes for URL-based workflows.

engine.description: The engine configuration to use during execution.

url.description:
Database URL (e.g., `sqlite://sprocket.db`). Defaults to `sprocket.db`
in the output directory. in the output directory.

MaxConcurrentRuns.description: Represents the maximum concurrent runs for the server.

anyOf.description: Do not limit the number of concurrent runs.
Use the specified maximum number of concurrent runs.

parallelism.description:
Number of test executions to run in parallel.

The default is `50`.

throttle.description:
Delay between submitting initial test executions, in milliseconds.

Once the `parallelism`` permits are exhausted, this throttle delay is
ignored and new tests are submitted eagerly as prior tests complete and
free permits.

The default is `100` milliseconds.

fixtures_dir.description:
Directory containing test fixture files.

If not set, fixtures are resolved from `<workspace>/test/fixtures`.

run_dir.description:
Directory to use for executing tests.

If not set, runs are written to `<workspace>/test/runs`.

index_page.description: Path to a Markdown file to embed in the `<output>/index.html` file.

logo.description:
Path to an SVG logo to embed on each page.

If not supplied, the default Sprocket logo will be used.

alt_light_logo.description:
Path to an alternate light mode SVG logo to embed on each page.

If not supplied, the `logo` SVG will be used; or if that is also not
supplied, the default Sprocket logo will be used.

homepage_url.description: An optional link to the project's homepage.

github_url.description: An optional link to the project's GitHub repository.

light_mode.description: Initialize pages in light mode instead of the default dark mode.

with_doc_comments.description:
Enables support for documentation comments

This option is *experimental*. Follow the pre-RFC discussion here: <https://github.com/openwdl/wdl/issues/757>.

extra_html.description: Configuration for custom HTML to embed in generated pages.

head.description:
Path to an HTML file that should have its contents embedded in each HTML
page, immediately before the closing `<head>` tag.

body_open.description:
Path to an HTML file that should have its contents embedded in each HTML
page, immediately after the opening `<body>` tag.

body_close.description:
Path to an HTML file that should have its contents embedded in each HTML
page, immediately before the closing `<body>` tag.

CommonConfig.description: Represents shared configuration options for Sprocket commands.

ColorMode.description: Represents the supported output color modes.

oneOf.description: Automatically colorize output depending on output device.
Always colorize output.
Never colorize output.

Mode.description: The diagnostic mode to use for reporting diagnostics.

oneOf.description: Prints diagnostics as multiple lines.
Prints diagnostics as one line.

WdlConfig.description: WDL-specific configuration options shared across all commands.

fallback_version.description:
The fallback version to use when a WDL document declares an
unrecognized version (e.g., `version development`).

FallbackVersion.description: Represents a fallback WDL version to use.

cache_path.description: Override the global cache location for this project.

large_file_warning.description:
Threshold for the large-file warning, or [`LargeFileWarning::Disabled`]
when the user opts out. Defaults to 1 MiB.

require_signed.description: Reject any unsigned module in the dependency tree.

trust_mode.description: TOFU policy for new signer keys.

allowed_schemes.description:
URL schemes permitted for top-level Git dependencies. Defaults
to `["https", "ssh"]`.

allowed_transitive_schemes.description:
URL schemes permitted for transitive Git dependencies. Defaults
to `["https"]` so remote manifests cannot silently trigger SSH
authentication against an attacker-controlled host.

max_advertised_refs.description:
Maximum number of advertised refs accepted from a remote.
Defaults to 100,000.

denied_hosts.description:
Hosts denied for all Git dependencies. Defaults to localhost
addresses.

allowed_hosts.description:
Hosts permitted for top-level Git dependencies. Empty means any
non-denied host is allowed.

allowed_transitive_hosts.description:
Hosts permitted for transitive Git dependencies. Defaults to
`["github.com", "gitlab.com"]`. When non-empty, a transitive
dependency may only be fetched from a host on this list, and Git
credentials are presented only to those hosts, so a transitive
manifest cannot direct the user's credentials at a host the user
has not vouched for. An empty list permits any non-denied host but
presents no credentials to transitive dependencies.

max_materialized_files.description:
Maximum number of files allowed in a single materialized module
tree. `None` (the default) disables the limit. Checked against
the Git tree object after fetch but before sparse checkout; this
bounds materialized content, not network transfer.

max_materialized_bytes.description:
Maximum total bytes of regular files allowed in a single
materialized module tree. `None` (the default) disables the
limit. Same enforcement point as `max_materialized_files`.

LargeFileWarning.description: Threshold for the large-file warning emitted at sign- and fetch-time.

anyOf.description: Files at or above this byte count trigger a warning.

TrustMode.description:
Trust-on-first-use (TOFU) policy for new signer keys.

When the resolver encounters a signed module whose signer key is not
yet recorded in the lockfile, this setting controls whether the key
is accepted silently or requires explicit user confirmation. The
library computes a [`LockfileDiff`](super::lock::LockfileDiff) that
flags new signers; the CLI is responsible for acting on the policy
(e.g., prompting the user when `Confirm` is set).

oneOf.description:
New signer keys are recorded in the lockfile without prompting.
This is the default and is suitable for non-interactive or
CI environments where manual confirmation is impractical.

The CLI must prompt the user to confirm any newly-trusted signer
key before writing the lockfile. Intended for interactive use
where the user wants to review each new signer.
